title: O Passado — Verbos Regulares
subtitle: Past Tense, Travel & Personal Experiences
description: Learn to talk about past events, holidays, and travel experiences using the Pretérito Perfeito of regular verbs.

vocabulary.example: No verão passado, viajei pela Europa.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Last summer, I travelled through Europe.
vocabulary.tip: Regular -ar verb. Past: viajei, viajaste, viajou, viajámos, viajaram. 'Uma viagem' = a trip/journey.

vocabulary.example: A viagem a Lisboa foi incrível!
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: The trip to Lisbon was incredible!
vocabulary.tip: Plural: 'as viagens'. 'Fazer uma viagem' = to take a trip. 'Boa viagem!' = Have a good trip!

vocabulary.example: Cheguei ao aeroporto com duas horas de antecedência.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I arrived at the airport two hours early.
vocabulary.tip: 'O aeroporto de Lisboa' = Humberto Delgado Airport. 'Fazer o check-in' = to check in.

vocabulary.example: Fiquei num hotel perto do centro histórico.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I stayed in a hotel near the historic centre.
vocabulary.tip: 'Ficar num hotel' = to stay in a hotel. 'Um hotel de quatro estrelas' = a four-star hotel. 'A pensão' = B&B/guesthouse.

vocabulary.example: Fui à praia todos os dias nas férias.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I went to the beach every day on holiday.
vocabulary.tip: Portugal has some of Europe's best beaches. 'Apanhar sol na praia' = to sunbathe on the beach. 'Tomar banho' = to swim.

vocabulary.example: Nas últimas férias, visitei o Porto e o Algarve.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: On my last holiday, I visited Porto and the Algarve.
vocabulary.tip: Always plural in Portuguese: 'as férias' (the holiday). 'Tirar férias' = to take holidays. 'Estar de férias' = to be on holiday.

vocabulary.example: Visitei o Museu dos Azulejos em Lisboa.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I visited the Azulejo Museum in Lisbon.
vocabulary.tip: Regular -ar verb. Past: visitei, visitaste, visitou, visitámos, visitaram.

vocabulary.translation: to meet / to get to know
vocabulary.example: Conheci pessoas fantásticas durante a viagem.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I met fantastic people during the trip.
vocabulary.tip: Regular -er verb (for past tense purposes). Past: conheci, conheceste, conheceu, conhecemos, conheceram.

vocabulary.example: Já visitaste Lisboa? É uma cidade maravilhosa!
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Have you already visited Lisbon? It's a wonderful city!
vocabulary.tip: 'Já' = already (affirmative) or yet (in questions). 'Já fui' (I've already gone). 'Já foste?' (Have you been yet?)

vocabulary.example: Ainda não visitei o Norte de Portugal.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I still haven't visited the North of Portugal.
vocabulary.tip: 'Ainda' = still (ongoing) or 'ainda não' = not yet. 'Ainda bem!' = Thank goodness! / Good thing!

vocabulary.example: Nunca provei bacalhau à Brás — quero experimentar.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I've never tried bacalhau à Brás — I want to try it.
vocabulary.tip: 'Nunca' = never. 'Nunca fui' (I never went). With 'não': 'Não fui nunca' = I never went. Both are correct.

vocabulary.example: Ontem comi um prato delicioso num restaurante local.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Yesterday I ate a delicious dish at a local restaurant.
vocabulary.tip: 'Ontem' = yesterday. 'Anteontem' = the day before yesterday. 'Ontem à tarde/noite' = yesterday afternoon/evening.

vocabulary.example: A semana passada fui ao cinema com a minha turma.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Last week I went to the cinema with my class.
vocabulary.tip: 'A semana passada' = last week. 'O mês passado' = last month. 'O ano passado' = last year.

vocabulary.example: No verão passado, passei duas semanas no Algarve.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Last summer, I spent two weeks in the Algarve.
vocabulary.tip: 'Passado/a' after a time word = last: 'o ano passado', 'o mês passado', 'a semana passada'.

vocabulary.example: Comemos muito bem em Portugal — a comida é deliciosa!
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: We ate very well in Portugal — the food is delicious!
vocabulary.tip: Regular -er verb. Past: comi, comeste, comeu, comemos, comeram. 'Comer fora' = to eat out.

vocabulary.example: Bebi vinho verde no Porto — é delicioso.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I drank green wine in Porto — it's delicious.
vocabulary.tip: Regular -er verb. Past: bebi, bebeste, bebeu, bebemos, beberam. 'Vinho verde' = young wine from northern Portugal.

vocabulary.example: Partimos de madrugada para apanhar o avião.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: We left at dawn to catch the plane.
vocabulary.tip: Regular -ir verb. Past: parti, partiste, partiu, partimos, partiram. 'Partir' also means 'to break (something)'.

vocabulary.example: Dormi muito bem no hotel — a cama era ótima.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I slept very well in the hotel — the bed was great.
vocabulary.tip: Irregular present (durmo, dormes...) but regular in past tense: dormi, dormiste, dormiu, dormimos, dormiram.

vocabulary.example: Viver em Portugal foi uma experiência incrível.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Living in Portugal was an incredible experience.
vocabulary.tip: 'Ter experiência' = to have experience. 'Uma experiência de vida' = a life experience.

vocabulary.example: Trouxe uma recordação de Lisboa para a minha família.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I brought a souvenir from Lisbon for my family.
vocabulary.tip: 'Recordação' = a memory (mental) or a souvenir (physical). 'Guardar uma recordação' = to keep a memory.

vocabulary.example: Adorei Lisboa — vou voltar com certeza!
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I loved Lisbon — I will definitely come back!
vocabulary.tip: Regular -ar verb. 'Adorei' = I loved it (stronger than 'gostei'). 'Adoro + infinitive' = I love doing...

grammar.title: Pretérito Perfeito — Past Tense of Regular Verbs

sections.heading: What Is the Pretérito Perfeito?
sections.body: The Pretérito Perfeito Simples (Simple Past) is the main past tense in Portuguese for completed actions. Use it when: (1) An action is COMPLETED and happened at a specific time in the past: 'Ontem comi pizza.' (Yesterday I ate pizza.) (2) An action happened a specific number of times: 'Fui a Portugal três vezes.' (I went to Portugal three times.) (3) A series of completed events: 'Acordei, tomei o pequeno-almoço e saí.' (I woke up, had breakfast, and left.) This is the most important past tense to learn first!

sections.heading: Pretérito Perfeito — Regular -AR Verbs (FALAR)
sections.caption: Other -AR verbs: comprei (bought), viajei (travelled), visitei (visited), adorei (loved)

sections.heading: Pretérito Perfeito — Regular -ER Verbs (COMER)
sections.caption: Other -ER verbs: bebi (drank), conheci (met), percebi (understood), vendi (sold)

sections.heading: Pretérito Perfeito — Regular -IR Verbs (PARTIR)
sections.caption: Other -IR verbs: dormi (slept), abri (opened), decidi (decided), ouvi (heard)

sections.heading: JÁ, AINDA (NÃO), and NUNCA with the Past Tense
sections.body: These three adverbs are essential for discussing past experiences: (1) JÁ = already (affirmative) or yet (questions): 'Já visitei Paris.' (I've already visited Paris.) 'Já foste a Lisboa?' (Have you been to Lisbon yet?) (2) AINDA NÃO = not yet: 'Ainda não comi bacalhau.' (I haven't eaten bacalhau yet.) (3) NUNCA = never: 'Nunca bebi vinho verde.' (I've never drunk green wine.) These adverbs tell us about whether an experience has or hasn't happened yet.

items.portuguese: No verão passado, viajei a Portugal e adorei!
items.translation: Last summer, I travelled to Portugal and loved it!

items.portuguese: Já visitaste o Museu do Azulejo? É fantástico.
items.translation: Have you already visited the Azulejo Museum? It's fantastic.

items.portuguese: Nunca comi tão bem como em Lisboa — a comida é incrível.
items.translation: I've never eaten so well as in Lisbon — the food is incredible.
items.pronunciation: NOON-kah koh-MEE tah-oo bem KOH-moo ehm...

items.portuguese: Ainda não fui ao Porto — quero ir no próximo inverno.
items.translation: I still haven't been to Porto — I want to go next winter.

sections.heading: Common Mistakes & Tips
items: Key distinction: -AR verbs → 'eu falei' (not 'falé'). The stressed syllable shifts: 'falar' → 'falámos', 'falaram'.
Don't confuse 'nós falámos' (we spoke — past) with 'nós falamos' (we speak — present). In EP, only the accent mark distinguishes them!
Use time markers to make it clear you're in the past: 'ontem', 'a semana passada', 'no verão passado', 'em 2023'.
'Já' in questions means 'yet': 'Já comeste?' (Have you eaten yet?). In statements, it means 'already': 'Já comi' (I've already eaten).
'Ainda não' = not yet. Don't say 'não ainda' — the correct order is 'ainda não'.

exercises.title: Conjugate in the Past Tense
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct Pretérito Perfeito form.

items.question: Ontem eu __ muito bem. (dormir)
items.explanation: 'Dormi' is the first person singular (eu) past tense of DORMIR (-IR verb): dormi, dormiste, dormiu, dormimos, dormiram.

items.question: A Ana __ um livro interessante. (ler → regular past)
items.explanation: 'Leu' is the third person singular (ela) past tense of LER: li, leste, leu, lemos, leram.

items.question: Nós __ o pequeno-almoço às 8h. (tomar)
items.explanation: 'Tomámos' (with accent) = we took (past tense). 'Tomamos' (no accent) = we take (present). The accent is key!

items.question: __ visitado Paris? (já — present perfect question)
items.explanation: 'Já' in a question = 'yet/already': 'Já visitaste Paris?' (Have you visited Paris yet?)

exercises.title: Past Travel Experiences
exercises.instructions: Complete with the correct past tense form.

items.sentence: Nas últimas férias, __ (viajar, eu) pelo Algarve.
items.fullAnswer: Nas últimas férias, viajei pelo Algarve.

items.sentence: Ainda não __ (visitar, eu) o Porto — quero ir em breve.

items.fullAnswer: Eles comeram muito bem no restaurante.

exercises.title: Past Tense Sentences
exercises.instructions: Click the correct word to complete each sentence.

items.sentence: No verão passado, ___ a Portugal com a minha família.
items.hint: past tense, 1st person singular of VIAJAR

items.sentence: ___ fui ao Porto — quero ir em breve.
